stories: Geopolitics back in the driver’s seat: U.S.–Iran tensions push oil up, tech wobbles.
Europe growth scare deepens: Germany Ifo slumps to 84.4 (lowest since May 2020) while Eurozone PMI dips into contraction with costs rising.
UK demand surprise: retail sales beat adds near-term support to growth expectations.
ECB April 30 preview: “fragile hold” as energy-driven inflation risk collides with weakening activity.
BOJ preview: steady rates likely, but sources see less hawkish messaging as fuel costs hit the outlook.
U.S. tape check: semis/PC complex in focus premarket (Intel, AMD among top movers).
ALPHA (Options): $TSM sees institution-style “upside + put monetization” structure flagged.
ALPHA (Deal/capital return): Santander pauses buyback *starting today* into Webster shareholder vote; restart window now defined.
Wildcard: Meme-style volatility is back—Avis Budget ($CAR) tied to a >400% short-squeeze move.
